pages.name: Home
pages.description: Public landing page for the dog club yearbook portal, introducing the yearbook and directing the user into the member area. Includes a prominent member login action that initializes the single-user session and routes into the dashboard, plus teasers of common workflows (dog profiles, ads, spreads, orders, sponsorships).
primary_functions: Introduce the yearbook project, submission deadlines, and key features of the portal
Provide clear calls to action to open the member dashboard and start submissions
Highlight quick links to common tasks like managing dogs, ads, spreads, and orders

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Member Login' button, successfully authenticate via memberLogin, then continue to the member dashboard
Click a 'How it works' or 'Help / FAQ' link from the homepage content
Click an 'About the Yearbook' teaser card or footer link
Click a 'Contact the Yearbook Team' link in the footer or hero area

access_methods.description: Accessible from the main header navigation as 'Home'
Default entry URL for the portal (main site URL)

pages.name: Member Dashboard
pages.description: Central hub for the single user to see an overview of their dogs, ads, spreads, proofs, orders, and sponsorships. Surfaces high-level stats and alerts, and provides quick navigation into all key management pages.
primary_functions: Summarize current yearbook submissions (dog profiles, ads, spreads, proofs, orders, sponsorships)
Provide quick-access links/buttons to My Dogs, Yearbook Ads, Layouts & Spreads, Proofs, Order Yearbook, and Sponsorships
Surface alerts such as pending proofs, incomplete profiles, and upcoming deadlines

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'My Dogs' quick action or card
Click 'Yearbook Ads' quick action or stats card
Click 'Layouts & Spreads' quick action or stats card
Click a 'View Proofs' button or a specific proof alert
Click 'Order Yearbook' call-to-action
Click 'Sponsorships' card or link from sponsorship summary
Click 'Need help with submissions?' link

access_methods.description: Accessible from the main header navigation as 'Dashboard'
Reached after successful login from Home

pages.name: My Dogs
pages.description: List and manage all dog profiles owned by the user, including status and bulk actions. Supports column visibility configuration, owner/status filters, inline status changes, and bulk locking of final profiles before yearbook submission.
primary_functions: Display a table of the user’s dog profiles with columns for name, section, status, and bio word count
Provide an 'Add New Profile' button that opens the dog profile creation form
Allow enabling/disabling table columns such as 'Bio Word Count' via a column settings control
Filter dog profiles by owner (pre-set to the single user) and other basic criteria
Allow changing each dog’s status between Draft and Final directly from the list
Provide bulk actions (such as 'Lock Final Profiles') applied to selected rows

incoming_params.description: Optional preset status filter (e.g., 'draft' or 'final') applied when arriving from dashboard shortcuts

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Add New Profile' button to create a new dog profile
Click a dog name in the table to view its profile detail
Click an 'Edit' action in a dog row

access_methods.description: Directly accessible from the 'My Dogs' header navigation link
Linked from dashboard summary cards and other contextual links
Optional 'status' query parameter can pre-filter the list

pages.name: Dog Profile Detail
pages.description: Detailed view of a single dog’s profile, including bio, activities, and achievements management. Provides tabs or sections for overview and achievements, and tools to add, filter, and sort achievements for that dog.
primary_functions: Display the dog’s core information such as name, breed, birth year, section, biography, and visibility
Show activities/tags associated with the dog (e.g., agility skills) in a read-only view
Provide navigation to edit the profile (link to dog_profile_edit.html)
Provide an 'Achievements' tab to list all achievements for the dog
Allow adding new achievements with fields for title, type, and date
Allow editing and deleting existing achievements as needed
Provide filters for achievements by year range (e.g., 2020–2024) and minimum number of events
Allow sorting achievements by date (oldest-first or newest-first) and saving the chosen order

incoming_params.description: DogProfile ID whose detail and achievements are displayed

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Edit Profile' button
Click 'Back to My Dogs' or similar breadcrumb navigation

access_methods.description: Requires 'id' query parameter to load a specific dog's profile
Accessed primarily via links from My Dogs or other contextual pages

pages.name: Dog Profile Edit
pages.description: Form for creating a new dog profile or editing an existing one for the yearbook. Includes fields for biography and activity tags with live word count and tag suggestions.
primary_functions: Create a new dog profile with fields for name, breed, birth year, section, and visibility
Edit existing dog profile fields, including updating section placement and visibility
Provide a biography text area supporting 120–150 word agility stories and other bios
Support adding and editing activity tags (e.g., 'Weave Poles', 'Jumping', 'Relay Teams') with typeahead suggestions
Validate required fields and display remaining/approximate word counts for the biography
Save the profile and return the user to either the My Dogs list or the dog’s detail page

incoming_params.description: Optional DogProfile ID to edit; if omitted and mode=create, a new profile is created
Indicates whether the form is in 'create' or 'edit' mode

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Save Profile' action that returns to the list after saveDogProfile succeeds
Click a 'Save and View Profile' action after saveDogProfile succeeds

access_methods.description: Uses 'id' and 'mode' query parameters to determine whether to load an existing profile or a blank form
Opened via 'Add New Profile' or 'Edit' links from My Dogs or Dog Profile Detail
After form submission, redirects to My Dogs or Dog Profile Detail based on user choice

pages.name: Yearbook Ads
pages.description: List and manage all yearbook advertisements associated with the user’s dogs. Provides visibility into sizes, costs, statuses, and any items needing attention.
primary_functions: Display a list of ads with columns such as dog name, ad size, cost, and status
Provide filters for ads by dog, size, status, or section if needed
Offer an 'Edit' link on each ad row to open the detailed ad configuration page
Summarize total expected ad spending for the current yearbook
Show quick indicators for ads that need text length updates or cost adjustments

incoming_params.description: Optional DogProfile ID used to pre-filter the list to ads for a specific dog

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Edit' link in an ad row

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Yearbook Ads' header navigation entry
Linked from dashboard ad summary and optionally from dog profile pages
Optional 'dogId' parameter can scope the listing to a particular dog

pages.name: Yearbook Ad Edit
pages.description: Configure the details of a single yearbook ad, including size, text, add-ons, and proofing options. Enforces pricing and word count guidance while showing real-time totals.
primary_functions: Allow selecting an ad size from a dropdown, with options such as Full Page, Half Page Classic, Half Page Premium, and Quarter Page with prices
Enforce business rules for choosing the cheapest qualifying option (e.g., cheapest half-page under a given budget)
Provide a text area for the ad copy, supporting 100–130 word tributes with live word count feedback
Offer add-on options (e.g., 'Foil Title', 'Extra Icons') as checkboxes, updating a real-time cost summary
Allow the user to enable or disable add-ons so that total cost stays under a desired budget such as $60
Provide dropdowns for placement preference (e.g., 'General Dog Tributes') and radio buttons for proofing method (e.g., 'Online Only')
Save changes and return the user to the Yearbook Ads list, showing the updated configuration

incoming_params.description: YearbookAd ID to edit; when present, loads existing ad configuration
Optional mode flag such as 'create' for initializing a new ad
Optional DogProfile ID to pre-associate when creating a new ad

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Save and Return to Summary' button after saveYearbookAd completes

access_methods.description: Uses 'id' for editing an existing ad or 'mode=create' (with optional dogId) for a new ad
Accessed via 'Edit' links from the Yearbook Ads list or dashboard
On save, navigates back to the Yearbook Ads list

pages.name: Layouts & Spreads
pages.description: Overview and management page for all layouts and multi-dog spreads in the yearbook. Shows existing spreads and provides entry points into the Spread Editor.
primary_functions: Display a list of existing spreads with information such as title, type (e.g., Two-Dog Feature), section, and status
Provide a 'Create New Spread' button to start a new spread in the Spread Editor
Allow users to open an existing spread in the editor for review or changes
Indicate which spreads are complete, pending content, or awaiting proof approval

incoming_params.description: Optional status filter (e.g., 'draft' or 'pending_proof') applied when navigating from dashboard shortcuts

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Create New Spread' button
Click an 'Edit' or spread title link in the spreads list

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Layouts & Spreads' header link
Linked from dashboard spread stats and other contextual shortcuts
Optional 'status' query parameter can pre-filter the spreads list

pages.name: Spread Editor
pages.description: Page for creating or editing a single layout spread, including two-dog feature configurations. Provides layout gallery filters, dog selection, and section assignment.
primary_functions: Allow selecting a spread type (e.g., 'Two-Dog Feature') for the new or existing spread
Display a layout gallery with filters such as 'Supports 2 Dogs' and 'Min Text 200+ words'
Provide sorting options like 'Price: Low to High' so users can select the cheapest layout meeting requirements
Show layout cards with details including supported dog count, text limits, and price
Allow selecting a layout card to apply it to the spread
Provide dropdowns to assign Dog 1 and Dog 2 (e.g., Milo and Luna) from existing profiles
Allow entering a spread title and selecting a section (e.g., 'Companions & Families')
Save the spread configuration and add it to the user’s yearbook submissions

incoming_params.description: Optional Spread ID to edit; when provided, loads an existing spread
Indicates 'create' or 'edit' mode for the spread editor

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Save Spread' after saveSpread succeeds to return to the spreads overview

access_methods.description: Uses 'id' and 'mode' query parameters to determine whether to load an existing spread or create a new one
Accessed from 'Create New Spread' or 'Edit' links on the Layouts & Spreads page
On save, returns to the Layouts & Spreads overview

pages.name: Proofs & Approvals
pages.description: List of all proofs (profiles, ads, spreads) awaiting review or already approved by the user. Provides filtering and quick access into detailed proof views.
primary_functions: Display a table of proofs with columns such as item type, dog name, status, last updated, and deadlines
Provide an 'Open Proof' action for each entry to view and comment on the proof in detail
Allow filtering proofs by type (profile, ad, spread) and approval status
Highlight proofs that require action, such as pending approvals or expiring auto-approval windows

incoming_params.description: Optional status filter (e.g., 'pending_review') to show a subset of proofs
Optional filter by type such as 'dog_profile', 'yearbook_ad', or 'spread'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Open Proof' on a proof row

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'Proofs & Approvals' header navigation link
Linked from dashboard proof alerts and other contextual notifications
Optional 'status' and 'itemType' parameters can pre-filter the list

pages.name: Proof Detail
pages.description: Detailed proofing interface for a single item, allowing comments, visibility controls, and approval decisions. Shows the rendered content, conversation thread, and approval options including auto-approval.
primary_functions: Display the full proof content (e.g., Bella’s profile or ad) as it will appear in the yearbook layout
Allow adding comments tied to specific sections (such as the main bio) with visibility options like 'Editors Only' or 'Private (Just Me)'
Display a threaded comment list showing all discussion on the proof with visibility indicators
Provide controls for setting approval options, including auto-approval conditions (e.g., auto-approve after 3 days without editor response)
Allow choosing an approval decision such as 'Approve as Is', 'Approve with Changes', or 'Request Revisions'
Submit the final approval request and record the decision and auto-approval settings

incoming_params.description: Proof ID whose detail, comments, and approval settings are displayed

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to Proofs & Approvals' after submitting or reviewing a proof

access_methods.description: Requires 'id' parameter to fetch and display the selected proof
Linked from the Proofs & Approvals list and dashboard proof alerts

pages.name: Order Yearbook
pages.description: Single-page, multi-step flow for ordering physical yearbooks, selecting shipping, and choosing payment method. Guides the member through edition selection, quantity, binding, shipping, and payment confirmation.
primary_functions: Allow selecting the yearbook edition (e.g., '2025 Club Yearbook') from a dropdown
Allow entering the quantity of yearbooks to order (e.g., 3 copies)
Provide binding options such as Softcover or Hardcover and allow choosing Softcover
Advance the user to a shipping options section with a 'Continue to Shipping Options' action
List available shipping methods with full order shipping costs, allowing selection of the cheapest option under a chosen budget (e.g., under $10)
Advance the user to the payment section where they can choose 'Pay Later / Invoice My Member Account' or other methods
Display an order summary including items, quantities, binding, shipping, and payment choice
Place and confirm the order, showing a confirmation message and order reference

incoming_params.description: Optional YearbookEdition ID to preselect a specific edition when starting an order

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Back to Dashboard' or 'View My Submissions' link after a successful order confirmation

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'Order Yearbook' header navigation link
Accessible via 'Order Yearbook' footer shortcut
Optional 'editionId' parameter can auto-select an edition in the order form
Order process involves multi-step form submission ending with placeYearbookOrder

pages.name: Sponsorships
pages.description: Manage sponsorship packages, assign them to dogs, and create dedication messages. Shows existing sponsorships and allows purchasing new packages under specific budget constraints.
primary_functions: Display the user’s existing sponsorships with package name, dog, and message
Provide a 'View Packages' area listing available sponsorship packages with names, descriptions, and prices
Offer sorting controls (e.g., sort by price low-to-high) to help find the most expensive package under a budget such as $75
Allow selecting a package (e.g., 'Bronze Paw Sponsor - $70') for purchase
Provide a form to assign the chosen sponsorship to a specific dog (e.g., Rex)
Allow entering a sponsor display name (e.g., 'The Miller Family') and a 30–40 word sponsor message
Confirm the sponsorship purchase and show a summary of the package and dedication

incoming_params.description: Optional DogProfile ID used to preselect the 'Assign to Dog' field when purchasing a sponsorship

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Back to Dashboard' link after confirming a sponsorship

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Sponsorships' header navigation item
Accessible via 'Sponsorships' footer shortcut
Optional 'dogId' parameter can pre-fill the sponsorship dog selection
Submitting the sponsorship purchase form triggers createSponsorship and shows confirmation

pages.name: About
pages.description: Information about the dog club, the yearbook project, and the purpose of this submission portal. Explains sections, timelines, and submission expectations for members.
primary_functions: Describe the dog club and its mission in relation to the yearbook
Explain how the yearbook is organized (sections, profiles, ads, spreads)
Set expectations about timelines, submission guidelines, and how content will be used

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Learn how to submit' or 'Read Help / FAQ' link
Click 'Contact the yearbook team' link for further questions

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'About' footer link
May also be linked from the Home page hero or introduction

pages.name: Help / FAQ
pages.description: Support and guidance for using the yearbook submission portal and completing typical tasks. Organized by workflow (profiles, ads, spreads, proofs, orders, sponsorships) with links back into the relevant pages.
primary_functions: Provide step-by-step help for key workflows like creating dog profiles, adding achievements, and placing orders
Answer common questions about word counts, image requirements, pricing, and deadlines
Offer troubleshooting tips and links back to relevant functional pages (My Dogs, Yearbook Ads, Proofs, Order Yearbook)

incoming_params.description: Optional key used to focus or scroll the page to a specific help topic or workflow

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Go to My Dogs' link inside a profile-management help topic
Click a 'Manage my ads' link in an ads-related FAQ
Click a 'Review my proofs' link in a proofing help topic
Click an 'Order your yearbook' link in an ordering help topic

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Help' in the header navigation
Accessible via 'Help / FAQ' in the footer
Optional 'topic' parameter can deep-link into a particular area of the help content

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact page for reaching the yearbook coordinators or support team. Shows contact details and offers a simple form that creates a support ticket or email request.
primary_functions: Display contact information such as email address and, if applicable, phone or mailing address
Provide a simple contact form for sending questions or support requests about the yearbook portal
Clarify expected response times and the types of inquiries that can be handled

incoming_params.description: Optional subject prefill for the contact form, based on where the user came from (e.g., 'Question about sponsorships')

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'View Help / FAQ' link if the user wants to consult documentation instead

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Contact' footer link
Linked from the Home and About pages for additional support
Submitting the contact form uses submitContactRequest and shows a confirmation state

header_links.description: Help and FAQs for using the portal

footer_links.description: About the club and the yearbook
Help resources and frequently asked questions
